Doctor's auth check was provider-blind: it only ever checked for an anthropic provider key, so configs targeting MiniMax (the default) silently passed validation and then died at first LLM call with SDK_AUTH_FAILED.
resolution: Load config + resolveProvider() in checkAuth() so it verifies the *resolved* provider's key, not a hardcoded one. Also surfaced the same provider-aware hint in the loop's SDK_AUTH_FAILED branch. Lesson: validators must mirror the resolution logic they validate.

name: tightly-scoped-builder-prompts
description: Builder prompts must be tightly scoped or MiniMax-M2.5 thrashes. Same task (sapling-3190, ~25 lines): open-ended prompt with 'follow code if it disagrees with spec' framing → 50 turns / 207k tokens / broken state at max-turns. Constrained recovery prompt naming the exact file, exact change, and 'do not run the gate more than twice' → 4 turns / 9k tokens / clean. The harness handles long runs fine; the failure mode is operator prompt design, not the loop.
